name: golang-github-sanity-io-litter-dev
title: golang-github-sanity-io-litter-dev
summary: Pretty printer library for Go data structures to aid in debugging and testing
description:
Pretty printer library for Go data structures to aid in debugging and testing
Litter is a pretty printer library for Go data structures to aid in
debugging and testing.
.
It's named for the fact that it outputs literals, which you litter
your output with. As a side benefit, all Litter output is compilable
Go. You can use Litter to emit data during debug, and it's also really
nice for "snapshot data" in unit tests, since it produces consistent,
sorted output.
.
Litter was inspired by Spew (https://github.com/davecgh/go-spew), but
focuses on terseness and readability.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-sanity-io-litter-dev because Pretty printer library for Go data structures to aid in debugging and testing.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-sanity-io-litter-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-sanity-io-litter-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-sanity-io-litter-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
